O loved of God! have a share in my penance! (Give me some wood!) 2. Incense burnt to avert the effects of an evil eye or to purify the air. dhūnī pānī kā sanjog , n. m. lit . meeting of fire and water. 1. Union. 2. Attachment; friendship. dhūnī denā , v. a. To fumigate medicinally, or to expel an evil spirit. Mirchoṅ kī dhūnī do, āp batā degā . Make a smoke of chillies, and the evil spirit will speak. dhūnī ramānā, yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To light a fire after the manner of faqīrs . 2. To become a jogī , 1. q. v.

s. f. 1. Persevering. 2. Smoke. 3. A fire lighted by a Hindū faḳīr, over which he sits, imbibing the smoke, by way of penance. Hence دهوني دينا dhūnī denā, To dun, to importune: to smoke. دهوني لگاني dhūnī lagānī, To insist obstinately, or to persevere in a demand. 4. ( met. ) Impudence. دهويا ديده dhoyā dīda, An eye void of shame, wanton, impudent. ديدي پهاڙنا dīde phāṛnā, v. a. To look steadfastly. ديده ريزي dīda-rez-ī, s. f. Fatiguing to the eye (as, minute objects, &c.). ديده کهولنا dīda kholnā, v. a. To consider maturely. ديده وان يا بان dīda-wān or -bān, The sight (of a gun, &c.). ديده و دانسته dīda-o-dānista, Wilfully, wittingly, purposely. ديده ودل سي dīda-o-dil se, adj. With heart and soul. ديده ور dīda-war, adj. Clearsighted. ديدي نيلي پيلي کرنا dīde nīle pīle karnā, To get into a passion or rage. (Plur. of sign. 1. ديدگان dīdagān ).
subs: adv. 'Having seen and known'; knowingly, wittingly, wilfully, purposely, intentionally.

ety: Pehl. dītan; rt. Zend did or dī = S. धी (दीधी)
def: v.t. To see, look, observe; to perceive, feel, experience; — seeing; experiencing, &c.
adj. To be seen, visible; fit or worthy to be seen.
more: adj. Fit or worthy to be seen.

ety: dī(dan), q.v.+dār = tār = Pehl. tar = Zend tar = S. तार्
def: s.m. Sight, vision ( = dīd); look, appearance; face, countenance, cheek; interview; — overseer, inspector, watch (syn. dīda-dār)
more: n. m. P. dīdan to see. See درشن 1—4. Khudā kā dīdār, na Mahammad kī shafāat . Prov. Nor the face of God, nor Mahommed's intercession. dīdār-bāz , n. One who ogles. dīdār-bāzī , n. f. 1. Sight-seeing; feasting one's eyes. 2. Ogling. Dīdār-bāzī, aur Maulā rāzī . Prov. The pleasure of the eye, with the approval of the Saint.
s. m. Sight, interview. ديدارخواه dīdār-ḵẖẉāh, Seeking or soliciting an interview.
subs: s.m.f., and dīdār-bāzī, s.f. = dīd-bāz, and dīd-bāzī, q.v.s.v. dīd
adj. & s.m. Seeking or soliciting an interview; — one who seeks an interview.

def: adj. Worthy of being seen, well-looking, fair, comely, handsome, personable, presentable.
more: adj. Good-looking; handsome; conspicuous. Baṛā dīdārū javān hai. dīd na shunīd , adj. lit . nor seen, nor heard. Strange; extraordinary; wonderful.

def: (for ديده بان dīda-bān, which has the same meaning) s. m. f. A watch, sentinel, scout.

def: n. f. Greediness; covetousness. Jau sasur kittoī khāë, pai ḍiḍyā lagāë rahatū hai . Rus. This child (called father-in-law by way of abuse) may eat ever so much, he is never satisfied.

more: n. m. S. दिक space, अम्बर covering. One whose only garment is the atmosphere. 1. An epithet of Shiv, or Durga. The place where you have naught to gain is not the place for you, What custom has the washerman where folks all naked go? 2. A sect of mendicant Jains who go about naked, as distinguished from Pitambars who wear colored clothes, and the Svetambars who wear white clothes only.

O leave my road, dear Sir! or in my eyes you'll threaded be! ḍagar batānā , v. n. To guids; direct one on his way.

more: E. n. m. A pole on which a load is suspended.

Each time with mighty strength he fiercely 'gainst him pressed, His dreadful tusks like radish snapped against his chest.
more: (lit. Elephant that supports one of the four quarters of the earth; met. ) Great.

def: n. f. Cor. of decree (حکم 6.). ḍigrī ijmālī . A joint decree. ḍigrī akhīr yā qataī , Final decree. ḍigrī iqbālī , n. A decree passed on confession of judgment; a judgment by cognovit. ḍigrī pānā, yā hāsil k. , v. a. To obtain a decree. ḍigrī jārī k . To enforce a decree; to take out execution. ḍigrī jārī karānā , v. a. To sue out execution. ḍigrī jārī karne-vālā , n. The party praying for execution of decree; the party who enforces a decree. ḍigrī-dār , n. Decree-holder; judgment creditor; the person in whose favour judgment has been given. ḍigrī sarsarī , n. f. A summary decree. ḍigrī se bachne kā upāë k . To elude or avoid judgment. ḍigrī shufٴa, yā haq-i-shufa . A decree. ḍigrī gair-vasūlī . An unsatisfied decree. ḍigrī-i-farebī , A fraudulent decree. ḍigrī k., yā denā , v. a. To adjudge; decree; award. ḍigrī ke ijrā meṅ , In the execution of decree. ḍigrī kī illat meṅ , On account of execution of decree. ḍigrī nāfiz k . To enforce a decree. ḍigrī yak-tarfah par muqadmah chhoṛnā , To suffer a default; let judgment go by default.
s.f. pl. (of ḍigrī), Degrees; — decrees.

more: 1. s. m. A village. 2. (in compos. from دادن) part. act. Giving, giver. 3. ¶ dah, adj. Ten. ده چند dah-chand, adj. Tenfold, ten times. ده چندگي dah-chanda-gī, s. f. Tenfold (a word with which they return a salutation). ده در دنيا ستردرآخرت dah dar dunyā sattar dar āḵẖirat, A benediction of faḳīrs, meaning, charity will be returned sevenfold in the next world. Brave, bold. ده روزه dah-roz-a, A ten days, a decade. ده مني dah-mann-ī, adj. Holding ten mann. ده يک dah-yak or ده يکي dah-yakī, Tenth part.

ety: fr. दिश्, q.v.; — euphonically changed to dig, in comp., before certain letters; and com. written dig in Hindī
def: s.m. (but fem. in S.), Point of the compass, quarter, direction, side, way; space, region, tract; (in comp.) -wards (e.g. uttar-dig, 'northwards')
more: n. m. P. musht . A blow with the fist. Aisā ḍuk dūṅgā kī dāṅt nikas paṛeṅge. ḍukkam ḍukkā, ḍukā ḍuk , n. f. Boxing; pugilism. Phr. A stand-up fight; a hand-to-hand fight. ḍukyā'nā , v. a. To give a blow.
subs: adj. & s.m. 'Sky-clothed'; having only the sky or atmosphere for raiment, clad by the regions of space, unclad, stark naked; — a naked person; a naked Hindū ascetic; an order of ascetics of the Jain sect who go naked (if clad in coloured cloth, they are called pītāmbar; if in white, svet-āmbar); a member of that order, a Jain mendicant; an epithet of Śiva (from his being naked), and of certain of his devotees
s.m. The end of space, the horizon, remote distance
s.m. Another region, a distant quarter; aërial space, the atmosphere
s.m. The functions of a dig-pāl or guardian deity of any region

dig-pati, s.m. The regent or guardian of one of the ten regions or points of the compass, or of a quarter of the world (of these guardian deities there are two divisions; 1˚ Astronomical, viz. the Sun, of the east; Saturn, of the west; Mercury, of the North; Mars, of the south; Venus, of the south-east; Rahū, of the south-west; the Moon, of the north-west; and Jupiter, of the north-east; — 2˚ Mythological, viz. Brahma, for upwards; Ananta, for downwards; Indra, of the east; Varuṇa, of the west; Kuvera, of the north; Yama, of the south; Agni, of the south-east; Nirriti, of the south-west; Marut or Vāyu, of the north-west; and Iʼśāna or Śiva, of the north-east)

dig-śūl, s.m. Any inauspicious planetary conjunction (as for the Sun and Venns to be in the west, &c.); see diśā-śūl, s.v. diśā; — dig-gaj, s.m. 'Elephant of the quarter'; a mythical elephant of one of the eight quarters or points (which, with others at different quarters, is supposed to support the world); (met.) great, large, huge
s.m. Point or quarter of the compass, point, quarter, direction
s.m. Subjugation of various countries in all directions; subjugation of extensive tracts of country (whether by arms or controversy), universal conquest
s.m. Invasion of various countries, going forth to conquer the whole world
s.m. Conqueror of many realms, or of the world
